label: Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation

node.label: Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation
node.definition: Knowledge learned in one task, domain, or modality is reused across others.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation is to connect Supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Sim-to-real, real-to-sim & digital transfer.
examples: substrate for Sim-to-real, real-to-sim & digital transfer
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation uses Embeddings & distributed representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
